title: Hidden Safety-Car / Rain-Crossover Regime Detector
summary: Official F1 strategy notes emphasize safety-car windows and driver-initiated pit calls in low-grip or wet transitions.
rationale: Official F1 strategy notes emphasize safety-car windows and driver-initiated pit calls in low-grip or wet transitions. The dataset lacks explicit SC/VSC/weather flags, so field-wide simultaneous slowdown and pit-burst patterns are likely missing latent drivers of pit timing.
implementation_hint: For each Race-Lap, derive robust field aggregates such as median/95th percentile LapTime_Delta, pit-stop count on current lap, share on wet tyres, and sudden jumps versus previous lap. Use these as gating features or train a separate 'neutralized/transition' specialist blended with the base dry-race model.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt mass-pit laps and reduce model confusion in wet/transition windows where tyre-age heuristics alone are weak.
risk: Race-lap aggregates can be noisy in sparse late-race situations and may leak if not computed fold-safely.
